body.title: [triage] Purge vestigial palace/home-DB psql write-paths from sprint-ticket + audit-debris
body.description:
**⛔ FAIL — KICKED BACK TO DESIGN 2026-07-13 (Akien: 'those should get sent back to the design step as FAIL for learning from and fixing').**

**THIS TICKET ENTERED SPRINT WITH NO INTENTION.** Under D-intentions-are-the-root-2026-07-13 and proof-on-close, THE INTENTION IS THE PROPERTY MUTATION-RED BREAKS. With no intention there is **no property to break**, so this ticket **cannot be proven as it stands** — it could only ever close hollow, or `shipped-unproven` for a reason that is not the real one.

**IT IS NOT DEFECTIVE WORK — IT IS INCOMPLETE DESIGN.** So the honest disposition is BACK TO DESIGN, not a backfilled intention. CC drafting the missing intention would have **papered over the real defect**: 150 tickets cleared the design step without an intention, which is a **gate hole**, not 150 individual oversights. Per CLAUDE.md, the nuisance stays VISIBLE so it drives finding the fix — never papered over, which sucks silently forever (CP4).

**CP2 — FAIL = FURTHER ADVANCE IN LEARNING.** This kickback IS the learning signal. The fix is upstream: the triage→sprint transition must reject a missing, empty, or degenerate (`TBD`/`unknown`) intention (T-sprint-tickets-with-no-intention-cannot-be-proven).

**TO RE-ENTER SPRINT:** state the intention as a present-tense contract — *'I intend that X, because Y'* — where X names a property a hollow build would violate.

**AND NOTE WHO FAILED HERE.** Akien wrote none of these tickets. **CC wrote all 149.** So 'the design step that failed' is CC, and the honest rule is not 'a human must declare it' — it is: **THE AUTHOR DECLARES THE INTENTION AT AUTHORING TIME, FROM THE CONVERSATION THAT PRODUCED THE TICKET.** That is what makes it `declared`: not that a human typed it (a ratified string and a machine-written string are byte-identical), but that it was stated from evidence INDEPENDENT of the artifact — the design conversation, which traces to Akien's intent. An intention RECONSTRUCTED LATER FROM THE TICKET BODY has no independent evidence and is `extracted-from-artifact`: a description, never a spec (D-provenance-is-the-safety-property-2026-07-13). **The failure was not forgetting a field. It was filing 149 tickets without ever pausing to say what I intended by them.**

---

**Problem:** Two skills still reference retired Postgres paths written before the palace->flat-file cutover: sprint-ticket:199 says 'INSERT via psql after approval' for storing palace nodes (the palace is retired; the canonical writer is memory_emit.py to the flat-file store), and audit-debris:54 runs 'psql $UU_HOME_DB_URL' for test-DB cleanup against a home DB that is vestigial. Akien confirmed both are vestigial ('the ticket was written before the change... we do it right and cleanup all the things').
**Proposed shape:** sprint-ticket — CLEAR-CUT: replace the 'INSERT via psql' palace-node path with the flat-file memory_emit.py path (or, if palace-node capture is itself retired, drop the step). audit-debris — CONTESTED, verify-before-delete: UU_HOME_DB_URL is still COMPOSED in the shell profile (bin/uu_bash_profile_processor.sh:45 builds postgresql://...), so the var is not dead; the question is only whether the test-schema-cleanup check is still meaningful (Akien: vestigial). BLOCKING precondition: confirm with Akien that no live home DB carries test schemas before deleting the check; if a home DB is live, keep it. (Separately note: unseen_university/__init__.py:3-6 is a botched no-op self-rename shim 'UU_HOME_DB_URL → UU_HOME_DB_URL' — out of scope here, flag for a follow-up.)
**Affected files:** skills/sprint-ticket/SKILL.md, skills/audit-debris/SKILL.md
**Design rules:** Filesystem memory store is canonical; memory_emit.py is the one write chokepoint. NOTE: Postgres-for-shared-state is still ALLOWED per CLAUDE.md — only the retired palace/home-DB-that-no-longer-exists paths are in scope here, not a blanket psql ban.
**Scope boundary:** Only the two named residual psql references; do not touch legit Postgres usage elsewhere.
**Test plan:** grep -rn 'psql' skills/sprint-ticket/SKILL.md skills/audit-debris/SKILL.md returns only intentional/negative refs (or 0); the palace-node path now routes through memory_emit.py.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
body.fail_reason: entered sprint with no intention — cannot be proven; design incomplete
